report_name: CrowdStrike: North Korea Stole Billions in Crypto, Financial Sector Attacks Up 43%

threat_actors.name: PRESSURE CHOLLIMA
threat_actors.description: A DPRK-nexus adversary responsible for the largest single financial theft ever recorded at $1.46 billion in 2025, achieved via a supply chain compromise with a trojanized application.

threat_actors.name: GOLDEN CHOLLIMA
threat_actors.description: A DPRK-nexus adversary that utilized sophisticated social engineering lures, including fake job recruitment offers, to compromise fintech companies in Southeast Asia and Canada, gaining access to cloud environments and diverting cryptocurrency. This group is noted for using AI to enhance its social engineering campaigns.
threat_actors.goals: Cryptocurrency theft and compromise of fintech companies.

threat_actors.name: MUTANT SPIDER
threat_actors.description: An eCrime syndicate that was a major driver of the surge in intrusions against the financial sector. This group specializes in vishing (voice phishing) to gain initial access and obtain credentials, which are then sold to ransomware operators.
threat_actors.goals: Initial access for ransomware operations and credential theft for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: Used by PRESSURE CHOLLIMA to conduct a record-breaking $1.46 billion cryptocurrency theft by compromising a software supply chain with a trojanized application.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: Used by GOLDEN CHOLLIMA in the form of recruitment-themed lures on platforms like LinkedIn to compromise fintech companies.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Voice
attack_patterns.description: Used as the primary initial access method by MUTANT SPIDER. The group conducts vishing (voice phishing) calls to employees, pretending to be IT support to trick them into giving up credentials or MFA codes.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used by attackers as the end goal of initial access techniques to access SaaS applications and cloud environments, blending in with normal user activity.

identities.name: Financial Services Organizations
identities.description: A class of organizations targeted by DPRK-nexus adversaries and eCrime syndicates, experiencing a 43% global increase in hands-on-keyboard intrusions. 423 were named on data leak sites.

identities.name: Fintech Companies
identities.description: A class of organizations in Southeast Asia and Canada targeted by GOLDEN CHOLLIMA for cryptocurrency theft.

indicators.name: Anomalous MFA Activity Indicative of Vishing
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Vishing Attempt via MFA Fatigue
id: 2f8b1c9a-7d3e-4b1f-8c9a-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a high number of MFA push notification rejections for a single user in a short time frame, which may indicate a vishing or MFA fatigue attack in progress. This pattern was highlighted in the CrowdStrike 2026 Financial Services report as a tactic used by groups like MUTANT SPIDER.
references:
  - https://www.crowdstrike.com/press-releases/crowdstrike-2026-financial-services-threat-landscape-report-reveals-dprk-nexus-adversaries-stole-billions/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-21
tags:
  - attack.t1621
  - attack.credential_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    ResultType: '500121' # MFA Denied
    ConditionalAccessStatus: 'failure'
  condition: selection | count(UserPrincipalName) > 5 by UserPrincipalName over 5m
falsepositives:
  - User accidentally denying multiple legitimate MFA prompts.
indicators.description: Detects an unusual number of MFA push notification rejections for a single user, which can indicate a vishing attack in progress, as described in the report.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous MFA Activity Indicative of Vishing
Anomalous MFA Activity Indicative of Vishing
